column1: # Tab-separated symbol file for the Teufel Cinebar One STM32F072 firmware.
# Types: FUNC, LABEL (code), DATA (RAM/flash struct), PERIPH (MMIO region)
# Use with Ghidra: open Script Manager → run the companion ghidra_load_symbols.py
# Or for other tools (IDA, Binary Ninja, custom scripts): parse this file directly.

column4: Infinite-loop trap; all unused IRQ vectors point here
Only application IRQ wired to a real handler
Wrapper — used by all 8 event-loop posters
strb r0,#3 → state[0] (intermediate during active entry)
strb r0,#4 → state[0] (intermediate during standby entry)
bl GPIO_WriteBit(GPIOF, 0x0001, 0) — DSP reset asserted (standby path)
bl GPIO_WriteBit(GPIOC, 0x8000, 0) — auxiliary signal off. NOP'd in firmware_06 (insufficient) and firmware_07
bl 0x0800C48C → set_PB7_LOW — KILLS audio rail. NOP'd in firmware_07
bl 0x0800C4EC → power-up sequence including PB7 reset pulse
4-instruction wrapper: bl 0x0800C9A0; called ONLY from transition_state standby path
Active-path power-up: PB7 init, pulse reset, probe-via-I2C, optional notify(0,19) + delay; called ONLY from transition_state active path
Pulses PB7 LOW for 30 ms then HIGH — classic PMIC reset
Main UI/event RTX thread; entry passed to osThreadNew at app_main init
bl 0x08011524 — runs when queue times out (auto-standby decision)
bl 0x0800ABE4 — posts type-7 event (intermediate auto-standby step)
bl notify(0, retval) — the broadcast Goal #1 needed
int notify(uint8_t channel, uint32_t value) — alloc msg pool, fill {channel, value}, post via osMessageQueuePut

uint8_t is_audio_active(void) — returns (GPIOA->IDR >> 3) & 1; reads PA3. ★ CORRECTION 2026-06-06: PA3 is dead-wired (always reads LOW) — this function always returns 0 in practice. Actual SPDIF activity is on PA4, not PA3. The firmware reads the wrong pin; auto_standby_check's silent-path therefore never fires.
uint8_t auto_standby_check(void) — checks audio activity vs elapsed time, returns 2 when sustained mode==1 (silent) > 1000 ticks
uint32_t GPIO_ReadPins(GPIO_TypeDef *port, uint32_t pin_mask) — used by is_audio_active
void GPIO_WriteBit(GPIO_TypeDef *port, uint32_t pin_mask, uint32_t value); used by transition_state
void vEEPROM_set_default(uint16_t key, uint16_t value) — set if not already persisted

column1: # === Event-loop posters (post {byte[0]=type, byte[4]=action} to event_loop queue) ===

column4: Posts {byte[0]=0, byte[4]=action}; 15 callers — THIS is how IR-toggle reaches event_loop
RTX timer callback for LED animation
Initializes g_system_state + creates RTX queues + spawns event_loop_thread
MOVS R1, #1 — sets state[0]=1 (standby) at boot
NVIC enable for EXTI0_1, EXTI2_3, EXTI4_15

Thin wrapper: push {r4,lr}; bl spdif_subsystem_init; pop {r4,pc}. Called from transition_state path 1 (active entry) AND path 2 (standby entry — at 0x0800A81A, the rail killer) and boot init at 0x0800A5D0.
Called near end of transition_state when going to active-stable (state[0]=2)
Enables GPIOA clock, writes PA2=LOW (BRR), reconfigs PA2 as Output_PP, reconfigs PA3 as Input. NOTE 2026-06-06: PA3 is the wrong pin (always LOW). PA4 is the actual SPDIF data line.
BL to spdif_subsystem_init_wrapper from transition_state path 2 — NOP'd in firmware_08+ to prevent PA2=LOW
BL GPIO_WriteBit(GPIOF, 1, 0) — PF0 LOW (DSP reset). NOP'd in firmware_17 to keep DSP alive in standby (gates PA4/Toslink module aliveness).
BNE 0x800acb4 — originally skipped auto_standby_check in non-active states. NOP'd in firmware_17 so the BL at +0x02 runs in all states (our shim dispatches).
BL auto_standby_check — redirected in firmware_17 to call our polling shim at 0x0801E820.
Real EXTI line 0,1 handler — installed via RTX5 VTOR relocation (static table at 0x54/0x58/0x5C all point to trap). Calls 0x800D4E4 for bits 0, 1.
Real EXTI line 2,3 handler. Calls 0x800D4E4 for bits 2, 3. ★ bit 3 dispatch goes to is_audio_active+0x8011590 but PA3 is dead so this fires rarely if ever.

Real EXTI line 4..15 handler. Calls 0x800D4E4 sequentially for bits 4 through 15. ★ bit 4 dispatch falls through (no case in dispatcher) — would be usable for PA4 wake IF PA4 didn't toggle at 5MHz biphase rate (too fast for EXTI).
Reads EXTI->PR, if r0-bit set: clears PR bit, calls exti_dispatch. Helper for the three EXTI_IRQ handlers.
Called from PA3 EXTI dispatch. Computes elapsed = now - last; stores last=now; records decision = 4 if elapsed > 450, else 1, else unchanged.

firmware_17 shim. 84 bytes. Dispatches by state[0]: active→auto_standby_check; standby→PA4 polling with debounce + silence_seen gate (state at 0x20002506) → post_event_type0(2) on transition. Other states→no-op.
Byte at +2 in g_auto_standby_state struct. NEW USE introduced by firmware_17 shim — 1 if last poll cycle observed all-LOW PA4, 0 otherwise. Drives the wake gate.
22-byte shim: orig init 2 + transition_state(2) + notify(0, retval)
2 KB vEEPROM emulation page 1 (active in baseline dump)
ASCII strings for named RTX objects
RTX5 kernel state struct; [+8]=kernel state byte, [+12]=tick count
Used by auto_standby_check; [+0]=last decision, [+1]=last mode, [+8]=last change tick
Holds notify's queue handle at [+12], mem pool at [+16]
